title: 4 CAS Online User Management
body_text:
4	CAS Online User Management
4.1	Overview of CAS Online User Management
This Chapter describes how to create, modify, and delete CAS Online users. Financial institutions can芒聙聶t assign access to CAS Online via CPS Admin or User Management. Central 1 manages all CAS Online users.
Users can access CAS online via a URL Central 1 provides to all new users, or they can access CAS Online via the Client Centre. If the user accesses CAS Online via the Client Centre, a Client Centre ID is required. See the CPS Admin Application Part or the User Management Part for procedures on creating Client Centre users. Although you can create Client Centre users, you can芒聙聶t assign access to CAS Online. See the following section for details.
4.2	Creating CAS Online Users
To create a CAS Online user:
Complete the CAS Online User Request, Form 2513. See Figure 1. Completion of the form is self-explanatory.
Important
:	Ensure that the CAS Online user ID isn芒聙聶t
the same as the individual芒聙聶s Client Centre username, if applicable.
Figure 1: CAS Online User Request, Form 2513
Send the completed form to Central 1 as directed on the form. Central 1 will communicate the one-time password directly to the CAS Online user and will provide a URL to CAS Online (for those who choose to access the application outside of the Client Centre).
4.3	Modifying CAS Online Users
To modify, unlock, or reset the password for a CAS Online user:
Complete the CAS Online User Request, Form 2513. See Figure 1. Completion of the form is self-explanatory.
Send the completed form to Central 1 as directed on the form. If a password reset is requested, Central 1 will communicate the one-time password directly to the CAS Online user.
4.4	Updating the CAS Online Password via the CAS Online Application
Changes to the CAS Online password don芒聙聶t affect the user芒聙聶s Client Centre password. Consider the following:
If the user doesn芒聙聶t know the CAS Online password, request a password reset. Submit the CAS Online User Request, Form 2513, to Central 1 as directed in Section 4.3, Modifying CAS Online Users.
If the user knows the CAS Online password and wants to change it, log in to CAS Online as directed in Section 5.1, Logging in to CAS Online, and select
Management
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Access the Management Tab
Select
Change Password
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Select Change Password
Enter the old password and the new password, then select
Submit
. See Figure 4.
The new password must be a minimum of eight characters in length and can contain alphanumeric and special characters, including capital letters. It can芒聙聶t be the same password used as one of the last six passwords.
All new passwords are checked against a list of known insecure passwords to prevent use of passwords that are known to have been exposed or shared from internet data breaches. Exposed passwords are more commonly used by hackers. It is recommended to use a pass-phrase as these are harder to guess (e.g., 芒聙聹deskchairphonemug芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹I LOVE ice Cream day芒聙聺).
Figure 4: Enter Password Details
4.5	Deleting CAS Online Users
To delete a CAS Online User:
Complete the CAS Online User Request, Form 2513. See Figure 1. Completion of the form is self-explanatory.
Send the completed form to Central 1 as directed on the form.
END
